_comment: Tier 3 TOOLS: Complete, hardcoded workflow implementations (executable Python functions). These are production-ready tools that users can call directly. For flexible guidance-only workflows, see knowledge/workflows/*.yaml

fetch_hydrological_data.description: Complete hardcoded function for fetching hydrological data from various sources (USGS streamflow, GridMET climate, etc.)
fetch_hydrological_data.purpose: Orchestrate data retrieval from USGS, climate databases, and other hydrological data sources in a single function call
use_when: User needs standard USGS streamflow data
User wants complete data package without customization
User doesn't need to modify data retrieval logic
fetch_hydrological_data.ai_guidance: This is a HARDCODED TOOL with a Python implementation. Use this when the user needs standard data extraction without modifications. The function handles all data fetching internally. If user needs custom filtering or specific data sources, consider recommending flexible workflow guidance instead.

site_number.description: USGS site number or gauge identifier

data_types.description: Types of data to fetch (streamflow, precipitation, temperature)

outputs.description: Dictionary containing all fetched datasets organized by type

fetch_hydrological_data.usage_example:
from ai_hydro.workflows.fetch_data import fetch_hydrological_data

data = fetch_hydrological_data(
    site_number='03335500',
    start_date='2000-01-01',
    end_date='2020-12-31',
    data_types=['streamflow', 'precipitation']
)

print(f"Streamflow: {data['streamflow'].shape}")

compute_signatures.description: Complete hardcoded function for computing comprehensive hydrological signatures (flow stats, timing indices, variability metrics)
compute_signatures.purpose: Calculate full suite of diagnostic signatures for watershed characterization in one function call
use_when: User wants all common flow statistics computed
User doesn't need custom signature definitions
compute_signatures.ai_guidance: This is a HARDCODED TOOL that computes predefined signature sets. Use when user needs standard hydrological signatures without modification. If user wants to compute only specific signatures or custom metrics, guide them to use individual Tier 2 tools from ai_hydro.tools.hydrology instead.

signature_sets.description: Sets of signatures to compute (flow, timing, variability, all)

outputs.description: Dictionary of computed signatures organized by category

compute_signatures.usage_example:
from ai_hydro.workflows.compute_signatures import compute_all_signatures

signatures = compute_all_signatures(
    streamflow=flow_df,
    precipitation=precip_df,
    signature_sets=['flow', 'timing', 'variability']
)

print(f"Mean flow: {signatures['flow']['q_mean']:.2f} cfs")

camels_extraction.description: Complete hardcoded function for extracting CAMELS-like catchment attributes (topography, climate, soil, vegetation, geology)
camels_extraction.purpose: Generate standardized catchment attributes following CAMELS dataset methodology
use_when: User needs complete CAMELS attribute set
All CAMELS attribute categories are needed
camels_extraction.ai_guidance: This is a HARDCODED TOOL that extracts the complete CAMELS attribute suite. Use when user needs standard CAMELS attributes without modifications. If user needs only specific attribute categories or custom attribute definitions, guide them to use individual Tier 2 tools (extract_topographic_attributes, extract_climate_indices, etc.) instead.

attribute_categories.description: Categories of attributes to extract (topography, climate, soil, vegetation, geology, all)

outputs.description: Dictionary of CAMELS attributes organized by category with units

camels_extraction.usage_example:
from ai_hydro.workflows.camels_extraction import extract_all_attributes

attrs = extract_all_attributes(
    watershed_boundary=watershed_gdf,
    outlet_coords=(40.4405833, -86.8291565),
    attribute_categories=['topography', 'climate', 'soil']
)

print(f"Mean elevation: {attrs['topography']['elev_mean']:.1f} m")

auto_modeling.description: Automated hardcoded workflow for hydrological model setup, calibration, validation, and analysis
auto_modeling.purpose: Complete automated modeling pipeline with standard algorithms
auto_modeling.ai_guidance: This is a HARDCODED TOOL for automated modeling. Use when user needs standard model calibration without customization. If user needs specific calibration algorithms, custom objective functions, or manual parameter control, this tool may be too rigid - guide them to use modeling framework directly.

model_type.description: Type of model to use (GR4J, SAC-SMA, HBV)

outputs.description: Model results including calibrated parameters, performance metrics, and predictions

auto_modeling.usage_example:
from ai_hydro.workflows.modeling import auto_modeling

results = auto_modeling(
    watershed=watershed_gdf,
    streamflow=flow_df,
    model_type='GR4J'
)

print(f"NSE: {results['performance']['nse']:.3f}")
